sections.summary: Title only — Standard for Gochujang (document title, non‑substantive).
Document identifier CXS 294‑2023 and adoption year 2023 (bibliographic information).

Applies to gochujang as defined in Section 2 when offered for direct consumption (including catering and repacking), excludes products intended for further processing and excludes chili paste/sauce where red pepper is the main ingredient.
Section heading only: Description (no substantive requirements in this item).

Defines gochujang as a red or dark red pasty fermented food produced by saccharifying grain starch (via powdered malt or safe naturally occurring microorganisms), mixing salt and fermenting/aging the mixture, adding red pepper powder and optional ingredients before or after fermentation, and applying heat or other appropriate treatment and hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage.
Section heading and overview for essential composition and quality factors (introduces subsections 3.1–3.4) rather than additional substantive requirements.
Section heading only: Composition.
Specifies basic ingredients of gochujang as grains, red pepper (Capsicum annuum L.) powder, salt and potable water.

Lists optional ingredients permitted in gochujang, including powdered meju (defined as fermented soybean or soybean‑grain material), soybeans, sugars (per CXS 212‑1999), distilled agricultural alcohol, soy sauce, fermented soybean paste, fish sauce (per CXS 302‑2011), seafood extract, fermented wheat protein, fermented rice, yeast extract, hydrolyzed vegetable protein, seasoned vegetables, vinegar and other ingredients.
Section heading only: Quality factors.
Sets compositional quality limits: capsaicin not less than 10.0 µg/mL (w/w), crude protein not less than 3.0% (w/w), and moisture not more than 60.0% (w/w).

sections.section: 3.2.2 Gochujang shall have its unique flavour, odour, and the following qualities.
sections.summary: Requires gochujang to have characteristic flavour and odour and specifies organoleptic qualities: red or dark red colour from Capsicum annuum L., a hot and savoury taste (may be somewhat sweet and somewhat salty), and an appropriate level of viscosity.
Product must be practically free from defects such as seeds, peels, grit or other extraneous matter.
Defines a 'defective' as any container that fails to meet the applicable quality requirements set out in Section 3.2.
A lot meets the quality requirements when the number of 'defectives' (per Section 3.3) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Permits certain acidity regulators, antioxidants, flavour enhancers, preservatives and stabilizers in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192‑1995) for food category 04.2.2.7, and requires flavourings to comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66‑2008).

sections.section: 4.1 Acidity  regulators,  antioxidants,  flavour  enhancers,  preservatives,  and  stabilizers  used  in  accordance  with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) 3   in food category 04.2.2.7 (fermented vegetable [including mushrooms and fungi, roots and tubers, pulses and legumes, and aloe vera] and  seaweed  products,  excluding  fermented  soybean  products  of  food  categories  06.8.6,  06.8.7,  12.9.1, 12.9.2.1 and 12.9.2.3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.
sections.summary: Permits use of acidity regulators, antioxidants, flavour enhancers, preservatives and stabilizers in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 04.2.2.7 (fermented vegetables and seaweed products, excluding specified fermented soybean products) in foods conforming to this standard.

sections.section: 4.2 The flavourings used in products covered by this standard should comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008). 4
sections.summary: Requires that flavourings used in products covered by this standard comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008).
Mandates that products comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Heading introducing hygiene provisions that recommend preparation and handling per the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and adherence to relevant Codex codes and microbiological criteria.

sections.section: 6.1 It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) 6   and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
sections.summary: Recommends that products be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.

sections.section: 6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997). 7
sections.summary: Requires that products comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
Heading for weights and measures provisions (no substantive requirements in this heading alone).
Heading for fill-of-container provisions (no substantive requirements in this heading alone).

Sets minimum fill requirements: sealed rigid containers must contain not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (allowing necessary head space per GMP), where water capacity is the volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container will hold, and flexible containers must be filled as full as commercially practicable.
Defines a container that fails to meet the minimum fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 as a 'defective'.
States that a lot meets the minimum fill requirement when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Specifies minimum weight tolerances by indicated weight: for ≤1,000 g tolerance <15 g; for 1,000–5,000 g net weight must be ≥98.5% of indicated weight; for >5,000 g net weight must be ≥99% of indicated weight.
Defines a container that fails to meet the minimum weight requirement of Section 7.2 as a 'defective'.
States that a lot meets the minimum weight requirements when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.
Heading for labelling provisions and states that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific labelling provisions apply.
Heading for product name provisions (no substantive requirement in this heading alone).

sections.section: 8.1.1 The name of product shall be ' gochujang '
sections.summary: Requires that the product name shall be 'gochujang'.

sections.section: 8.1.2 The name of product can be labelled in accordance with domestic laws, so that its characteristics may be expressed
sections.summary: Permits the product name to be labelled in accordance with domestic laws so that the product's characteristics may be expressed.
Requires labelling of non-retail containers to be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Requires that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance be those contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions of this standard.
